safety_notes: Peaceful and crime-free. The danger is environmental — altitude, cold, road conditions, remoteness. Not a security concern, a survival concern.

reach.from_nearest_city: Manali → Rohtang → Kunzum → Kaza (200km, 8-10hrs). OR Shimla → Kinnaur → Kaza (400km, 2 days).
reach.road_condition: Manali side: NH3 to Atal Tunnel good, then deteriorates badly. Kunzum approach is unpaved gravel. Kinnaur side: NH5 along Sutlej is paved but landslide-prone.
reach.public_transport: HRTC bus Manali-Kaza (seasonal, Jun-Oct). HRTC bus Shimla-Kaza via Reckong Peo (year-round but unreliable in winter).
reach.self_drive: 4WD strongly recommended. High-clearance vehicle mandatory for Kunzum approach. Fuel up at Manali (Manali side) or Reckong Peo (Kinnaur side) — no fuel in between.

sleep.booking_method: Call ahead or walk-in. Zostel bookable online. Most homestays via WhatsApp or direct phone.
sleep.emergency_stay: Monastery guest houses will take you in if you're stranded. Ask at Key or Tabo.

fuel.next_after_that: Reckong Peo (210km toward Shimla) or Manali (200km back)

weather_night.note: Freezing EVERY night of the year above 3800m. Even in July, temperatures drop to -2°C at night. Carry thermals, a down jacket, and a sleeping bag liner even in summer.

emergency.nearest_hospital: Community Health Centre Kaza (basic). Nearest real hospital: Regional Hospital Kullu (6-10 hours).
emergency.rescue: ITBP posts at multiple locations along the route. Nearest major: ITBP Koksar.
emergency.ambulance: 108 (works on BSNL only, signal in Kaza/Tabo only)

network.note: BSNL is the ONLY network. Works in Kaza and Tabo. Zero signal in Langza, Hikkim, Komic, Dhankar, Pin Valley, Chandratal. Download offline maps before entering Spiti.

people_who_help.name: Spiti Ecosphere
people_who_help.note: Best resource in Spiti. Can arrange homestays, guides, transport, and help in emergencies. Run by locals.

people_who_help.name: Zostel Spiti
people_who_help.note: Staff know the area well and can help coordinate if you're stuck.

safety_notes: Standard tourist town safety. Petty theft in crowded areas. Avoid isolated spots at night. Main risk is road accidents and monsoon landslides, not crime.

reach.from_nearest_city: Delhi → Manali: 530km, 12-14hrs by road (Chandigarh-Mandi-Manali). Volvo overnight buses are the most popular option.
reach.road_condition: NH3 is a national highway — mostly good. Mandi-Manali stretch gets congested and landslide-prone in monsoon.
reach.public_transport: HRTC Volvo from Delhi (12hrs, overnight). HRTC ordinary from Chandigarh. Local buses to Solang, Old Manali, Naggar.
reach.self_drive: Any car will do. Parking is a nightmare on Mall Road — use hotel parking.

sleep.booking_method: All platforms: MakeMyTrip, Booking.com, Zostel, OYO. Walk-in possible off-season.
sleep.emergency_stay: Never an issue — hundreds of options at all price points.

fuel.nearest_petrol_pump: Multiple pumps in Manali town and on NH3
fuel.next_after_that: Tandi (40km toward Keylong via Atal Tunnel)

weather_night.note: Pleasant year-round compared to higher destinations. Snows Dec-Feb. Summer evenings are cool and comfortable.

emergency.nearest_hospital: Lady Willingdon Hospital Manali, Regional Hospital Kullu (40km). Multiple private clinics.
emergency.rescue: Manali has mountain rescue teams. SDRF Himachal Pradesh.

network.wifi_available: Most hotels, cafes in Old Manali
network.note: Full 4G coverage in Manali town. Signal weakens beyond Solang and toward Atal Tunnel.

people_who_help.name: Manali Tourism Office
people_who_help.note: Can help with transport, guides, and emergency coordination.

safety_notes: One of the safest hill stations in India. Strong police presence (it's the HP winter capital). Large international community. Women solo travelers regularly feel safe here.

reach.from_nearest_city: Delhi → Dharamshala: 480km, 10-12hrs. Pathankot rail then 3hr drive. Gaggal airport (15km) has flights from Delhi.
reach.road_condition: NH154 from Pathankot is well-maintained. McLeod Ganj is 9km uphill from Dharamshala — steep but paved.
reach.public_transport: HRTC Volvo from Delhi (overnight). Local buses from Pathankot, Chandigarh. Shared taxis McLeod-Dharamshala frequent.
reach.self_drive: Any car. Parking in McLeod is limited — use Dharamshala base if driving.

sleep.booking_method: All platforms. Chonor House and Norling House (Tibetan-run) book via their websites. Tushita meditation centre books via email.
sleep.emergency_stay: Walk-in always possible. Gurudwara accommodation available free.

fuel.nearest_petrol_pump: Dharamshala town (lower). None in McLeod Ganj.

weather_night.note: McLeod gets occasional snow in winter. Summer is pleasant. Monsoon (Jul-Aug) is very wet — heaviest rainfall in HP.

emergency.nearest_hospital: Zonal Hospital Dharamshala (modern, well-equipped). Delek Hospital McLeod Ganj (Tibetan, basic but good).
emergency.police_station: McLeod Ganj Police Station. Dharamshala Kotwali.
emergency.rescue: Mountain rescue available through local police.

network.wifi_available: Most cafes and hotels. Moonpeak Espresso has good wifi.
network.note: Full coverage in McLeod and Dharamshala. Weakens on Triund trek above treeline.

people_who_help.name: Tibet Bureau
people_who_help.note: Can help with cultural questions and local connections.

people_who_help.name: Lha Charitable Trust
people_who_help.note: Runs volunteer programs, deeply connected to local community.

safety_notes: One of the safest valleys in Himachal. No tourist scams, no crowds, no crime. The biggest risk is the river in monsoon — respect it.

reach.from_nearest_city: Chandigarh → Aut Tunnel turn-off → Gushaini: 280km, 6-7hrs. Turn right at Aut tunnel (off NH3 before Kullu).
reach.road_condition: NH3 to Aut is national highway (good). Aut to Gushaini is state road — narrow, steep in places, but paved. Fine for any car in dry weather.
reach.public_transport: HRTC bus to Aut/Banjar. From Banjar, shared taxis to Gushaini. Very infrequent — plan ahead or hire.
reach.self_drive: Any car is fine. The road from Aut narrows significantly — drive slowly around bends. Night driving NOT recommended.

sleep.booking_method: Most homestays via direct phone/WhatsApp. Raju's Cottage books months ahead. Himalayan Trout House on Google.
sleep.emergency_stay: Homestay owners will always find you a bed. Community is tight-knit and welcoming.

weather_night.note: Mild compared to higher Himachal. Comfortable even in summer evenings. Carry a light jacket always.

emergency.nearest_hospital: PHC Banjar (basic). Regional Hospital Kullu (2 hours).
emergency.rescue: Forest Department (GHNP) rangers can help in park area.

network.note: Decent signal in Gushaini and Banjar. Patchy in Jibhi. Zero inside GHNP trek routes.

people_who_help.name: Raju Bharti (Raju's Cottage)
people_who_help.note: The original Tirthan homestay. Raju knows every trail, every local, every fishing spot. Call him for anything.

people_who_help.name: GHNP Rangers
people_who_help.note: Mandatory for park treks. Can coordinate rescue inside the park.

safety_notes: Very safe. Army presence near the border. Locals are welcoming. The only risk is the road (narrow, cliff edges on Sangla approach) and altitude.

reach.from_nearest_city: Shimla → Narkanda → Rampur → Karcham → Sangla → Chitkul: 370km, 10-12hrs. Break journey at Kalpa or Sangla.
reach.road_condition: NH5 along Sutlej to Karcham is paved but landslide-prone in monsoon. Karcham-Chitkul is narrow, paved, cliff-edge sections. No barriers in many places.
reach.public_transport: HRTC bus Shimla to Sangla (seasonal). From Sangla, shared taxi to Chitkul. Very infrequent.
reach.self_drive: High-clearance vehicle preferred. Careful driving required on Karcham-Chitkul stretch. NOT recommended in monsoon for inexperienced drivers.

sleep.booking_method: Zostel Chitkul online. Others via phone/walk-in. Limited options — book ahead in May-Oct.
sleep.emergency_stay: Village is small but locals will accommodate you. Army camp nearby can help in extreme emergency.

fuel.nearest_petrol_pump: Sangla (25km). Actually, closest reliable pump is Karcham (40km).

weather_night.note: At 3450m, nights are cold year-round. Winter is brutal — most places shut Nov-Mar. Carry warm sleeping bag even in June.

emergency.nearest_hospital: PHC Sangla (basic). District Hospital Reckong Peo (2 hours). Real hospital: Rampur (5-6 hours).
emergency.police_station: Sangla Police Station. Army presence near Chitkul.
emergency.rescue: ITBP/Army posts near border can assist.
emergency.ambulance: 108 (unreliable in this area — long response time)

network.wifi_available: Zostel has basic wifi. Most places none.
network.note: BSNL only. Patchy even in Chitkul village. Better in Sangla. Download offline maps before Karcham.

people_who_help.name: Zostel Chitkul staff
people_who_help.note: Well-connected to village locals. Can help arrange onward transport or emergency assistance.

safety_notes: No crime risk. Danger is environmental: monsoon conditions, slippery trails, leeches, altitude. The approach road (Joshimath area) has had subsidence issues since 2023.

reach.from_nearest_city: Rishikesh → Devprayag → Rudraprayag → Joshimath → Govindghat: 270km, 10-12hrs. Then 13km trek to Ghangaria (or helicopter).
reach.road_condition: NH58 to Joshimath is the Char Dham route — heavily trafficked, landslide-prone in monsoon. Govindghat to Ghangaria is foot only.
reach.public_transport: Bus Rishikesh-Joshimath-Govindghat. Pony/porter from Govindghat to Ghangaria. Helicopter (weather dependent).
reach.self_drive: Drive to Govindghat, park. From there it's a trek. The drive itself is long and tiring.

sleep.booking_method: GMVN guest house bookable online (book early for Aug). Gurudwara accommodation free (basic, first-come). Private guest houses walk-in.
sleep.emergency_stay: Gurudwara at Ghangaria always has space and free langar (meals).

weather_night.note: Open only Jul-Sep when it's monsoon. Expect rain every day. Nights at 3658m are cold even in August — carry warm layers and rain gear.

emergency.nearest_hospital: PHC Govindghat (very basic). District Hospital Joshimath (20km from Govindghat). Nearest major: Srinagar Garhwal (6hrs).
emergency.rescue: Forest Department at valley gate. SDRF Uttarakhand.
emergency.ambulance: 108 (to Govindghat only — no vehicles beyond that)

network.note: BSNL only at Govindghat, nothing at Ghangaria or in the valley. You are completely offline for 2-3 days.

people_who_help.name: GMVN Guest House staff
people_who_help.note: The de facto info centre at Ghangaria. Staff know trail conditions.

people_who_help.name: Gurudwara Hemkund Sahib committee
people_who_help.note: Free meals and basic accommodation. Will help anyone regardless of religion.

safety_notes: No crime. The danger is real and environmental: altitude sickness, extreme crowds, flash floods, landslides. The 2013 disaster killed thousands. Monsoon yatra is genuinely risky.

reach.from_nearest_city: Rishikesh → Rudraprayag → Guptkashi → Sonprayag → Gaurikund: 220km, 9-10hrs. Then 16km trek or helicopter from Phata/Sersi.
reach.road_condition: Char Dham highway — heavy traffic in season. Well-maintained but monsoon brings landslides. Sonprayag-Gaurikund is narrow and congested.
reach.public_transport: Bus Rishikesh-Guptkashi. Shared taxi/shuttle Guptkashi-Sonprayag-Gaurikund. Trek or pony or helicopter from Gaurikund.
reach.self_drive: Drive to Sonprayag (Gaurikund road is restricted to public vehicles). Park and take shuttle.

sleep.booking_method: GMVN online. Most tents/dharamshalas are walk-in. For comfort, stay in Guptkashi and day-trip.
sleep.emergency_stay: Temple premises always have space. Dharamshalas near temple are free/donation-based.

weather_night.note: At 3583m, nights are always cold. Even in peak May-June season, temperatures drop to 3-5°C at night. Carry thermals and a warm jacket.

emergency.nearest_hospital: Medical camp at Kedarnath (seasonal, basic first aid). District Hospital Rudraprayag (80km from Gaurikund). Air ambulance helicopter possible in clear weather.
emergency.rescue: SDRF Uttarakhand. NDRF deploys during yatra season.
emergency.helpline: Char Dham Yatra helpline: 1364. Uttarakhand SDMA: 1070

network.wifi_available: None at Kedarnath. Guptkashi has coverage.
network.note: BSNL works intermittently at Kedarnath. All networks work in Guptkashi. No signal on the 16km trek.

people_who_help.name: Kedarnath Temple Committee
people_who_help.note: Coordinates with government on crowd management and emergency response.

people_who_help.name: SDRF Uttarakhand
people_who_help.note: Deployed during yatra season. Can coordinate helicopter evacuation in emergencies.

safety_notes: Safe tourist destination. ITBP and Army presence in the area. Main concern is Joshimath land subsidence — check advisories. Monsoon road risk on approach.

reach.from_nearest_city: Rishikesh → Devprayag → Rudraprayag → Joshimath: 250km, 10-12hrs. Then cable car or 14km drive to Auli.
reach.road_condition: Char Dham highway to Joshimath. Road is okay but long. Joshimath to Auli is a winding mountain road — cable car is easier.
reach.public_transport: Bus Rishikesh-Joshimath. Cable car Joshimath-Auli (year-round, weather permitting).
reach.self_drive: Drive to Joshimath, take cable car. Or drive up to Auli (14km, steep switchbacks).

sleep.booking_method: GMVN online. Cliff Top Club and private hotels on MakeMyTrip/Booking.com. Joshimath has cheaper options.
sleep.emergency_stay: Hotels at Auli or Joshimath. GMVN always has rooms off-season.

weather_night.note: At 2800m, proper snow in winter (Dec-Feb). Summer is pleasant but cool at night. Ski season: Jan-Feb.

emergency.nearest_hospital: Military Hospital Joshimath (may accept civilians in emergency). District Hospital Chamoli (70km).

network.wifi_available: Most hotels at Auli and Joshimath
network.note: Good coverage at Joshimath and Auli. Weakens on Gorson Bugyal trek.

people_who_help.name: GMVN Auli Resort
people_who_help.note: Runs ski courses and knows the mountain well. Good first point of contact.

people_who_help.name: ITBP Joshimath
people_who_help.note: Run ski training courses. Can assist in mountain emergencies.

safety_notes: Safe area. Less trafficked than Char Dham route. Main risks: altitude at Chandrashila (4000m), cold weather, and the monsoon trail conditions.

reach.road_condition: Good until Ukhimath. Ukhimath to Chopta is a mountain road — narrow but paved. Less affected by Char Dham traffic.
reach.public_transport: Bus Rishikesh to Ukhimath/Gopeshwar. From Ukhimath, shared taxi to Chopta. Infrequent — hire recommended.
reach.self_drive: Any car. Road is manageable. Parking at Chopta trailhead is limited.

sleep.booking_method: Forest Department guest house bookable. Most cabins/tents are walk-in or phone. More options at Sari village (for Deoria Tal).
sleep.emergency_stay: Dhaba owners at Chopta can arrange emergency shelter. Sari village has more options.

weather_night.note: At 2680m (Chopta), manageable. At 4000m (Chandrashila), it's bitter cold with wind chill. Winter snow trek requires proper gear.

emergency.rescue: Forest Department rangers on the Tungnath trail.

network.wifi_available: None at Chopta. Sari village has some.
network.note: Jio works at Chopta meadow. No signal on the Tungnath/Chandrashila trek above treeline.

people_who_help.name: Forest Department Chopta
people_who_help.note: Can advise on trail conditions and assist in weather emergencies on the trek.

safety_notes: Peaceful, remote Kumaon town. Zero crime concern. Main risk is the long approach road and remoteness — limited medical facilities.

reach.from_nearest_city: Kathgodam (railhead) → Almora → Chaukori → Thal → Munsiyari: 300km, 12-14hrs. Break journey at Almora or Chaukori.
reach.road_condition: Mix of state highways and mountain roads. Almora to Munsiyari via Thal is narrow with hairpin bends. Berinag route alternative. Both are long.
reach.public_transport: KMOU bus from Kathgodam (rare, very slow). Shared taxis from Almora. Best to hire a vehicle.
reach.self_drive: Possible in any car but the drive is exhausting. Break it into 2 days. Night driving is dangerous on these roads.

sleep.booking_method: KMVN Tourist Rest House online (view-facing, reliable). Sarmoli homestays via Mountain Shepherds Initiative. Most others via phone.
sleep.emergency_stay: KMVN always has rooms. Sarmoli community will arrange emergency accommodation.

fuel.nearest_petrol_pump: Munsiyari town (one pump, sometimes runs dry)

weather_night.note: At 2200m, climate is more moderate than Spiti/Ladakh. Snow possible Dec-Feb. Pleasant in summer. Always carry a warm layer for evenings.

emergency.rescue: SDRF Uttarakhand. Army base nearby (near border).

network.wifi_available: KMVN and some hotels have basic wifi
network.note: Jio and BSNL work in Munsiyari town. Zero signal on Khaliya Top trek and in surrounding villages like Sarmoli (BSNL patchy).

people_who_help.name: Mountain Shepherds Initiative (Sarmoli)
people_who_help.note: Run homestays, guides, bird walks. Deeply embedded in local community. Best local resource.

people_who_help.name: KMVN Tourist Rest House
people_who_help.note: Staff know the area. Can help arrange transport and local contacts.
